dataSourceNote: The structured data pipeline was unreachable again this session. Unlike prior HOLD days, today's process seriously evaluated a specific candidate (GOOGL, post-earnings) rather than treating data imprecision as an automatic veto — the reasoning documents why it still didn't clear the bar.

webSearchFindings.GOOGL: Q2 2026 earnings (reported 2026-07-22): revenue $119.8B (+24% YoY), Google Cloud $24.8B (+82% YoY), EPS $9.11 vs $2.88 consensus (large beat, likely includes non-operating items). Stock fell ~5% in after-hours trading on raised 2026 capex guidance to $205B (from ~$190-195B) with 2027 spending flagged to rise further. Current-price sources conflicted: one cited a $347.15 close (-1.38%) and separately implied ~$351.99 previous close; another cited $350.89 premarket. Cross-checked via the 52-week-high framing ('$408.61, current price 17.7% below high') which backs out to ~$347.16, triangulating toward ~$347 as the more reliable estimate. Coverage explicitly frames this as 'Alphabet declined over the past month amid rising AI infrastructure costs' — an ongoing month-long trend, not a single-day panic. Analyst consensus Strong Buy, 12-month target $433.55 (+24.8% from ~$347). Source: quiverquant.com, macrotrends.net, stocktwits.com, stockmarketwatch.com (via WebSearch, cross-referenced due to inconsistency).
webSearchFindings.TSLA: Q2 2026 earnings (reported 2026-07-22 after close): revenue $22.5-28.24B (sources vary, +25.5-26% YoY, a beat), but EPS $0.32-0.33 missed the $0.50-0.54 consensus by ~39%, gross margin missed, and free cash flow turned negative. Stock fell ~5% during the earnings call to below $357 (from ~$377-379 pre-earnings). A genuine profitability/quality deterioration, not just a sentiment-driven overreaction to good numbers. Source: gurufocus.com, tradingkey.com, Yahoo Finance/Motley Fool (via WebSearch).
webSearchFindings.AAPL: Closed $325.89 on 2026-07-22 (-0.56%, day range $323.34-$329.00), continuing to consolidate within its stop ($308) / take-profit ($338) band. 'Apple Upgrade' device-leasing program launching 2026-07-28 to boost sales; Broadcom chip-supply deal reaffirmed. Source: WebSearch aggregated finance/news pages.
webSearchFindings.NVDA: Up 3.26% on 2026-07-22 (prior close $207.29, +1.97%), helped by $60B+ in server orders, a new $700M US plant, Wistron's new Fort Worth facility, and improving sentiment as the Fed's rate path becomes clearer — an early stabilization sign for the semiconductor sector, though not yet confirmation of exiting the technical bear market flagged 2026-07-21. Source: ts2.tech, tradingkey.com (via WebSearch).
webSearchFindings.SPY/QQQ: Roughly flat at midday Wednesday 2026-07-22 (SPY +0.19%, QQQ +0.08%), with oil rising (WTI ~$88, US conducting an 11th consecutive night of strikes on Iran) and the 10-year Treasury yield up slightly to 4.65% — mild risk-off tone, not a sharp move. Source: Benzinga, TipRanks (via WebSearch).

AAPL.status: Consolidating around $325.89 (2026-07-22 close) — comfortably within the stop/target band, held unchanged.

decisionProcess: In direct response to user feedback that recent decisions have defaulted to HOLD without real engagement, this decision explicitly evaluated GOOGL as a post-earnings buy-the-dip candidate rather than reflexively citing data imprecision. Findings: GOOGL's earnings beat was real and strong (Cloud +82%, revenue +24%), but the stock's weakness is an ongoing month-long decline driven by a persistent, unresolved capex/FCF concern (2026 capex raised to $205B) rather than a one-off panic that has since stabilized — and current-price sources disagreed by ~1-2% even after cross-referencing, which is too much slippage to size a defensible stop-loss/take-profit on. TSLA's post-earnings drop reflects genuine profitability deterioration (EPS miss, negative FCF, margin compression), not a sentiment overreaction, so it was ruled out as a dip-buy on fundamentals rather than data-quality grounds, and shorting into a highly volatile post-earnings session with an already-realized move was judged poor risk/reward regardless. The existing AAPL position continues unchanged, still within its original stop/target band.

entries._dataAsOf: 2026-07-22 (web search fallback — structured pipeline unreachable this session, see dataSourceNote)
